raw_user_request: Improve local-project-harness as a multi-agent orchestrator policy and skill validation harness.
concrete_goal: Update local-project-harness policy and skill validation rules for multi-agent orchestration so contracts, review reports, and runner checks distinguish harness governance work from end-user feature work.
success_criteria: The policy rules describe how multi-agent orchestration contracts, review independence, lifecycle closeout, and runner checks should be validated.
Schema and policy examples keep classification separate from harness, orchestrator, agent lifecycle, and skill documentation changes.
Runner sync and README sync checks continue to pass after the policy fixture updates.
scope_in: Harness policy examples, schema-policy validation text, and targeted fixtures for multi-agent orchestrator behavior.
Validation checks that protect review lifecycle, delegation budget, and runner policy sync behavior.
scope_out: End-user feature implementation, runtime previews, and presentation deliverables.
Changes to unrelated source files or external service integrations.
assumptions: The work improves a local validation harness and not a shipped end-user deliverable.
Multi-agent terminology refers to orchestration policy and lifecycle governance, not an operator runtime.
implementation_constraints: Keep changes scoped to local-project-harness policy, script, schema, or fixture files.
Do not weaken end-user evidence requirements while fixing orchestrator policy classification.

scenario_flows.name: Classify harness orchestrator policy work as schema policy
preconditions: A Goal Contract describes local-project-harness multi-agent orchestration policy work.
steps: Run goal-logic on the schema_policy contract.
Inspect whether classification inference treats bare agent terminology as an end-user noun.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The contract remains schema_policy because it is about harness governance, runner checks, and lifecycle policy rather than end-user feature implementation.
failure_or_edge_cases: Bare agent or multi-agent wording must not trigger an end-user runtime class when no explicit end-user runtime context is present.
Orchestrator policy and lifecycle language should remain schema_policy even when it discusses subagents and review workers.

verification_matrix.build: Run syntax checks for changed validation scripts.
verification_matrix.lint: Run available static validation if present.
verification_matrix.test: Run goal-logic fixtures covering classification boundaries.
verification_matrix.run: Not applicable for schema_policy harness work; no user-facing runtime is produced.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Verify the multi-agent orchestrator policy fixture passes goal-logic while mislabeled creation fixtures still fail.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Record node --check output for the harness validation script.
Record goal-logic output for this schema_policy orchestrator fixture.
Record invalid fixture failures for the known mislabeled creation and improvement requests.
Record runner-policy-sync and README sync output.

iteration_policy.threshold_rule: overall_completion_score must be at least quality_score_threshold.
iteration_policy.rework_rule: Any failed validation command requires a targeted fixture or script correction before acceptance.
stop_conditions: A pre-existing unrelated dirty worktree change blocks sync verification.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Update local-project-harness validation policy without treating multi-agent orchestrator wording as an end-user deliverable.
